opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: [Nurse reports] He has been incredibly thirsty for days, urinating constantly, and today he doesn't even know his own name and can barely open his eyes.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: Progressive confusion and severe weakness over the past week in a known Type 2 diabetic.

symptom_narrative.character: profound thirst and urination initially, now severe confusion and lethargy
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: not applicable — patient confused and unable to reliably self-report
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: gradually worsening over about a week
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: massive polyuria and polydipsia earlier in the week, now confused, disoriented, extremely drowsy, no nausea/vomiting/abdominal pain reported
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: known Type 2 diabetes on metformin and gliclazide, medication compliance poor this past week due to lethargy, possible untreated urinary tract infection suspected
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: bedbound, unable to feed or hydrate independently

current_medications_as_reported.dose_as_patient_reports: regular, though compliance poor this past week due to lethargy

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: nursing home resident, facility nursing staff present and providing history

psychosocial_profile.hidden_agenda: nursing staff concerned about the insidious decline and want guidance on urgency
